questions.question: Let X and Y be two discrete random variables. If P(X=0, Y=0) = 0.1 and P(X=0) = 0.2, what is P(Y=0)?
questions.hint: Use the definition of joint probability distribution

questions.question: For two discrete random variables X and Y, if P(X=1, Y=1) = 0.3 and P(X=0, Y=1) = 0.2, what is P(X=1) + P(Y=1)?
questions.hint: Use the definition of joint probability distribution and marginal probability

questions.question: If the joint probability distribution of two discrete random variables X and Y is given by P(X=1, Y=1) = 0.2, P(X=1, Y=0) = 0.1, P(X=0, Y=1) = 0.3, and P(X=0, Y=0) = 0.4, what is the value of P(X=1) + P(Y=1)?
questions.hint: Use the properties of joint probability distribution
